message:
fix(db): drop partial-index predicate + canonical-open the checkpoint path + add runtime-followup test

Three small but related database-layer changes landed together:

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs
- Migration v16_idx_atc_experiences_open: drop the
  `WHERE state = 'open'` partial-index predicate. FrankenSQLite's
  runtime build currently rejects partial indexes created via this
  migration path during re-open (the predicate's AST reconstruction
  path doesn't round-trip cleanly yet), so the migration would
  succeed once but any subsequent franken-runtime open would fail.
  The non-partial index is strictly a superset of the partial one
  — it still satisfies the
  `SELECT … FROM atc_experiences WHERE state = 'open'` lookup the
  partial index was created for, just without the write-time
  row-elision. Size delta is negligible for this table's
  cardinality.
- New regression test
  `runtime_canonical_followup_keeps_franken_runtime_openable`:
  applies each migration from
  `schema_migrations_runtime_canonical_followup()` in turn on a
  canonical (rusqlite-style) connection, then verifies a
  franken-runtime `DbConn::open_file` still succeeds after each
  migration lands. Would have caught the partial-index regression
  above before it shipped.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs
- `wal_checkpoint_truncate_path` previously wrapped
  `open_sqlite_file_with_lock_retry(...)` in
  `guard_db_conn(..., "wal checkpoint connection")`. That wrapper
  keeps the connection on the generic DbConn guardrails but routes
  PRAGMA work through the path where franken might try to parse
  reconstructed DDL (which is the same class of issue the partial
  index hit above).
- Switch to
  `open_sqlite_file_with_lock_retry_canonical(path_str.as_ref())` so
  the checkpoint path always goes through the canonical connection —
  faster, and sidesteps partial-index / other AST-roundtrip corner
  cases the canonical path doesn't care about.
- PRAGMA busy_timeout / wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) behaviour unchanged.
